sanskrit: किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तम् इच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुम् अहं तथैव। तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेन सहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते॥
transliteration: kirīṭinaṃ gadinaṃ cakra-hastam icchāmi tvāṃ draṣṭum ahaṃ tathaiva | tenaiva rūpeṇa catur-bhujena sahasra-bāho bhava viśva-mūrte ||
literal_meaning: With crown, with mace, with discus in hand—thus I desire to see You. By that very four-armed form, O thousand-armed One, O Universal Form, please become (so).
contextual_meaning: After being overwhelmed by the terrifying omniform, Arjuna requests a return to a bounded, benevolent, familiar icon that his mind can hold without dissociating.
psychological_state: Post-awe regression to secure base; shift from hyper-arousal to organized attachment figure.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack or psychedelic overwhelm, begging the therapist to 'just sit still and look normal again.'
anchor_text: When infinity terrifies, ask for a face you can love.
